output: Batch 2 complete (T2, T3, T5, T14). T2: `repairConfig` now detects legacy `start`+`children` pinned groups and converts to soft-tag shape (deriving clipIds from children, otherwise preserving existing clipIds); migrate.test.ts adds 4 cases covering load conversion, no-children fallback, save round-trip emitting no legacy fields, and idempotent no-op. T3: `clonePinnedShotGroups` (in timeline-data.ts) now copies only soft-tag fields; `buildTimelineData` and `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` no longer call `projectPinnedShotGroups`; `render-bounds.validation.test.ts` fixture updated. T5: `pinned-group-projection.ts` reduced to `findEnclosingPinnedGroup`, `categorizeSelection`, and the new `orderClipIdsByAt` helper; all 11 projection-era helpers deleted; projection test rewritten with 10 surviving-helper cases; `pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts` deleted. T14: new `lib/interaction-state.ts` module with createInteractionState/isInteractionActive/onInteractionEnd/notifyInteractionEndIfIdle backing a `{drag, resize, listeners}` shape; useTimelineState creates the ref via that helper; `useTimelinePersistence.scheduleSave` now stashes deferred payloads + flushes newest on gesture end; `usePollSync` extends gate with `interactionActive` rejection reason + stashes deferred polled data + re-evaluates via `interactionEndTick` on end; `useClipDrag` and `TimelineCanvas` resize-end now call `notifyInteractionEndIfIdle`. Regression tests added: interaction-state.test.ts (6 cases), useTimelinePersistence.test.tsx (4 cases — drag/resize defer + newest-payload flush + baseline), usePollSync.test.ts extended with interactionActive cases. **30 tests across 6 files pass.** No new TS errors in any T14-touched file; remaining tsc errors are T1/T5 cascade scheduled for T6–T11. No newly-added diagnostic console.logs were found in `git diff HEAD`; pre-existing dev-only [TimelineSync]/[TimelineSave] telemetry left intact.
deviations: Did not run the full video-editor vitest suite: T1+T5 intentionally remove fields/exports that dozens of existing test files (and many runtime files) still depend on; the suite produces cascading failures explicitly scheduled for T6–T11 and T15. Ran instead the 6 test files directly modified or added by this batch — all 30 tests pass. The persistence hook test file is brand new (no existing useTimelinePersistence test file) and was added specifically as the deferred-save regression test required by T14.

Diagnostic console.log audit: searched git diff HEAD for newly-added console.log lines in src/tools/video-editor/** — found none. The remaining [TimelineSync]/[TimelineSave] logs in persistence/poll are pre-existing dev-only telemetry, not the 'temporary debugging' logs the plan flagged. Left them alone. If reviewer disagrees on which logs are 'temporary', point them out and I can remove them in a follow-up batch.

task_updates.executor_notes: repairConfig in src/tools/video-editor/lib/migrate.ts now detects legacy projection-shape pinned groups (start and/or children) and converts to soft-tag shape, deriving clipIds from children when present and preserving existing clipIds otherwise. Removed the buildGroupFromConfigClips import. Added migrate.test.ts with 4 cases: legacy {start+children} converts and strips legacy fields; legacy {start} only without children preserves clipIds; round-trip via configToRows -> rowsToConfig emits hasOwnProperty('start')===false and hasOwnProperty('children')===false; already-soft-tag config is unchanged. All 4 tests pass.

clonePinnedShotGroups in timeline-data.ts now copies only soft-tag fields (shotId, trackId, clipIds, mode, videoAssetKey, imageClipSnapshot). rowsToConfig pipes pinnedShotGroups through this clone helper, so its output is soft-tag only with no projection step. Removed projectPinnedShotGroups import + call from buildTimelineData and from buildDataFromCurrentRegistry in timeline-save-utils.ts. Updated render-bounds.validation.test.ts:153-161 to expect the soft-tag shape (no start, no children). render-bounds.validation.test.ts (6 tests) passes.

src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-projection.ts replaced with a minimal soft-tag-only file exporting findEnclosingPinnedGroup, categorizeSelection, and the new orderClipIdsByAt(clipIds, {clips? rows?}) helper. All projection-era helpers (projectPinnedShotGroups, resizeGroupEdge, resizeChildBoundary, replaceGroup, replaceGroupChildren, buildGroupFromChildren, buildGroupFromConfigClips, normalizeChildren, setClipTimelineDuration, moveGroup, getGroupDuration) deleted. Rewrote pinned-group-projection.test.ts with 10 focused cases for the surviving helpers (lookups, categorization, orderClipIdsByAt covering clips/rows context, unknown ids, stable order on equal at). Deleted pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts outright. Cascading import errors in useClipDrag/useClipResize/useExternalDrop/usePinnedShotGroups/useSwitchToFinalVideo/useTimelineCommit/useTimelineTrackManagement/multi-drag-utils are explicitly scheduled for T6-T11.

Added src/tools/video-editor/lib/interaction-state.ts with createInteractionState() returning {drag,resize,listeners:Set<()=>void>}, isInteractionActive(ref), onInteractionEnd(ref,cb) returning unsubscribe, and notifyInteractionEndIfIdle(ref) which fires all listeners only when both flags are false. useTimelineState now creates the ref via createInteractionState. useTimelineSave/useTimelinePersistence/usePollSync all use the shared InteractionStateRef type. 

useTimelinePersistence.scheduleSave: when isInteractionActive(ref) is true at call time, the newest payload is stashed in deferredSaveRef and the 500ms timer is NOT started. A useEffect-registered onInteractionEnd listener flushes the stashed payload via the same scheduleSave path on gesture end (passes the gate this time). 

usePollSync: TimelinePollGate/PollRejectionInput extended with optional interactionActive. isTimelinePollIdle returns false when interactionActive. getTimelinePollRejectionReason returns 'interaction active' as a new rejection reason. The poll-acceptance effect reads isInteractionActive into the gate; rejected payloads are stashed in deferredPolledDataRef and an interactionEndTick state bump (registered via onInteractionEnd) re-runs the effect on gesture end so the latest payload is reconsidered. 

useClipDrag drag-end (line 162-163) and TimelineCanvas resize-end (line 796-799) now call notifyInteractionEndIfIdle(ref) after flipping the flag to false. 

Diagnostic console.log audit: searched `git diff HEAD` for newly-added console.log lines in src/tools/video-editor/** — none found. Existing [TimelineSync]/[TimelineSave] logs are pre-existing dev-only telemetry per repo conventions (CLAUDE.md notes debug logs are dev-only stripped in prod). Left them alone.

Regression tests added/extended: (a) src/tools/video-editor/lib/interaction-state.test.ts (6 cases): active flags, listener lifecycle, multicast, error isolation, unsubscribe. (b) src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePollSync.test.ts: 2 new cases for interactionActive=true rejection ('interaction active') and interactionActive=false acceptance. (c) src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelinePersistence.test.tsx (NEW file, 4 cases): drag-active scheduleSave does NOT call saveTimeline after 2s; resize-active scheduleSave does NOT call saveTimeline after 2s; the newest of three deferred payloads (drag-3) flushes on notifyInteractionEndIfIdle and earlier payloads are discarded; baseline normal scheduleSave still fires after the 500ms debounce. All 16 tests pass. Targeted tsc on tsconfig.app.json reports zero new errors in persistence, poll, save, state, contexts, types, interaction-state — only T1/T5 cascade in unrelated downstream files.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed. repairConfig detects legacy groups via `typeof start === 'number' || Array.isArray(children)`, derives clipIds from children when present (preserves existing clipIds otherwise), and emits soft-tag-only output. migrate.test.ts covers (a) load conversion with both start+children, (b) load conversion with only start (no children) preserving clipIds, (c) round-trip via configToRows -> rowsToConfig asserting hasOwnProperty('start')===false and hasOwnProperty('children')===false on the saved output, (d) idempotent no-op for already-soft-tag configs. All 4 tests pass.

Confirmed. clonePinnedShotGroups in timeline-data.ts copies exactly { shotId, trackId, clipIds, mode, videoAssetKey, imageClipSnapshot } \u2014 no start/children clone branches. rowsToConfig pipes pinnedShotGroups through clonePinnedShotGroups, so its output is soft-tag only. buildDataFromCurrentRegistry in timeline-save-utils.ts no longer imports or calls projectPinnedShotGroups; the migrateToFlatTracks(repairConfig(...)) chain is the only transform. buildTimelineData in timeline-data.ts no longer calls projectPinnedShotGroups either. render-bounds.validation.test.ts fixture updated to expect soft-tag shape (no start, no children); test passes.

Confirmed. pinned-group-projection.ts now exports only findEnclosingPinnedGroup, categorizeSelection, and the new orderClipIdsByAt helper (plus PinnedGroupKey and CategorizedSelection types). All 11 projection-era helpers deleted (projectPinnedShotGroups, resizeGroupEdge, resizeChildBoundary, replaceGroup, replaceGroupChildren, buildGroupFromChildren, buildGroupFromConfigClips, normalizeChildren, setClipTimelineDuration, moveGroup, getGroupDuration). pinned-group-projection.test.ts rewritten with 10 focused cases for the surviving helpers. pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts deleted from disk.

Confirmed. SAVE: useTimelinePersistence.scheduleSave gates on isInteractionActive(interactionStateRef) \u2014 when active, the newest payload is stashed in deferredSaveRef and no timer is started; an onInteractionEnd-registered useEffect listener calls scheduleSave on the deferred payload when the gesture ends (the call passes the gate the second time). useTimelinePersistence.test.tsx 'flushes the newest deferred payload after the gesture ends' proves drag-1/drag-2/drag-3 collapse to a single saveTimeline call carrying drag-3. POLL: getTimelinePollRejectionReason now returns 'interaction active' when interactionActive is true; usePollSync injects isInteractionActive(ref) into the gate, stashes deferred polled data in deferredPolledDataRef, and an interactionEndTick state bump (registered via onInteractionEnd) re-runs the acceptance effect on gesture end. usePollSync.test.ts new cases verify the rejection and acceptance reasons. CONSOLE.LOGS: audited git diff HEAD for newly-added console.log lines in video-editor \u2014 none found; existing dev-only telemetry left intact. TESTS: 16 deferred-save/poll/listener regression tests across interaction-state.test.ts, usePollSync.test.ts, and useTimelinePersistence.test.tsx all pass.
